content.text:
All done. Here's what was built:

---

**New: `16_Agents/Orchestrator Agent/`**

| File | What it does |
|---|---|
| `README.md` | Purpose + 4-tier orchestration sequence (post-pull daily · deep research weekly · backtesting Friday · conviction review monthly); full command set for `opportunity-viewpoints`, `agent-analyst`, `disclosure-reality`, `quant sim`, `sports-calibration` |
| `Pulls.md` | 6 sections: Priority Queue (🔴🟠 cross-domain) · Watch Queue · Opportunity Viewpoints · Agent Analysis · Disclosure Reality · Backtest Results |
| `Sources.md` | Full-vault source inventory — queries all `01_Data_Sources` (synthesis agent owns everything) |
| `Signals.md` | Cross-Agent Signal Board (all 06_Signals sorted by severity) · Thesis Confirmation Queue · Requires Action (alerts with no `related_theses` yet) |

---

**New: `00_Dashboard/Main Dashboard.md`**

Section order (agent flow first):

1. **Agent Pipeline Status** — dataviewjs live table: all 16 agents, last pull date, 🔴🟠🟡⚪ per agent
2. **Orchestrator Priority Queue** — top 20 critical/alert items cross-domain
3. **Signal History** — last 15 from `06_Signals`
4. **Investment Research Feed** — opportunity-viewpoints + agent-analysis outputs
5. **Thesis-Linked Evidence** — pulls with `related_theses` + elevated signal
6. **System Health** — dataviewjs stale-agent detector (>3 days)
7. **Quick Launch** — full pull → orchestration chain → deep dive commands
8. **Navigation** — links to all key vault layers

The LMT note you opened (`RSI 19, below 200DMA, alert`) will surface immediately in the Priority Queue since `signal_status = "alert"`.
Okay run a full cycle with the Orchestrator

The user wants to run the full Orchestrator post-pull sequence:
1. `opportunity-viewpoints --window 14`
2. `agent-analyst --all-thesis --skip-llm`
3. `thesis-canvas`

Let me run these in background as a logged script.
Running the full Orchestrator post-pull sequence in background.

content.title:
cd "/c/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts" && cat > /tmp/orchestrator-run.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
LOG="/tmp/orchestrator-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M).log"
cd "/c/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts"

run() {
  echo "=== $1 ===" >> "$LOG"
  shift
  node run.mjs "$@" 2>&1 | tail -10 >> "$LOG"
  echo "" >> "$LOG"
}

echo "Orchestrator cycle started: $(date)" >> "$LOG"
echo "LOG=$LOG"

# Step 1 — Cross-source opportunity scan
run "opportunity-viewpoints" pull opportunity-viewpoints --window 14

content.newText:
---
title: Main Dashboard
type: dashboard
tags: [dashboard, main, orchestrator, agents]
last_updated: 2026-04-28
---

# Main Dashboard

![[sitdeck]]

---

## Agent Pipeline

```dataviewjs
const routes = [
  ["Orchestrator", ["05_Data_Pulls/Orchestrator"], ["orchestrator"]],
  ["Market",       ["05_Data_Pulls/Market"],       ["market", "agent-analysis"]],
  ["Macro",        ["05_Data_Pulls/Macro"],         ["macro"]],
  ["Thesis",       ["05_Data_Pulls/Theses"],        ["thesis"]],
  ["Fundamentals", ["05_Data_Pulls/Fundamentals"],  ["fundamentals"]],
  ["Biotech",      ["05_Data_Pulls/Biotech"],       ["biotech"]],
  ["Government",   ["05_Data_Pulls/Government"],    ["government"]],
  ["Housing",      ["05_Data_Pulls/Housing"],       ["housing"]],
  ["Energy",       ["05_Data_Pulls/Energy"],        ["energy"]],
  ["Research",     ["05_Data_Pulls/Research"],      ["research", "arxiv", "pubmed"]],
  ["Sectors",      ["05_Data_Pulls/Sectors"],       ["sectors", "sector"]],
  ["Sports",       ["05_Data_Pulls/Sports"],        ["sports"]],
  ["OSINT",        ["05_Data_Pulls/osint", "05_Data_Pulls/social"], ["osint", "social"]],
  ["VC",           ["05_Data_Pulls/VC"],            ["vc", "capital-raise", "deal-flow"]],
  ["News",         ["05_Data_Pulls/News"],          ["news", "media", "sentiment"]],
  ["Legal",        ["05_Data_Pulls/Legal"],         ["legal", "rulemaking", "regulatory"]],
];

function inAnyFolder(page, folders) {
  return folders.some(f => page.file.folder === f || page.file.folder.startsWith(f + "/"));
}
function hasAnyTag(page, tags) {
  const vals = Array.isArray(page.tags) ? page.tags.map(t => String(t).toLowerCase()) : [];
  return tags.some(t => vals.includes(t));
}

const rows = routes.map(([agent, folders, tags]) => {
  const pulls = dv.pages('"05_Data_Pulls"')
    .where(p => inAnyFolder(p, folders) || hasAnyTag(p, tags));
  const critical = pulls.where(p => p.signal_status === "critical").length;
  const alert    = pulls.where(p => p.signal_status === "alert").length;
  const watch    = pulls.where(p => p.signal_status === "watch").length;
  const latest   = pulls.sort(p => p.date_pulled, "desc").first();
  const status   = critical > 0 ? "🔴" : alert > 0 ? "🟠" : watch > 0 ? "🟡" : "⚪";
  const lastPull = latest ? latest.date_pulled : "—";
  return [status, agent, lastPull, critical || "—", alert || "—", watch || "—", latest ? latest.file.link : "—"];
});

dv.table(["", "Agent", "Last Pull", "🔴", "🟠", "🟡", "Latest"], rows);
```

---

## Priority Queue

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  choice(signal_status = "critical", "🔴",
    choice(signal_status = "alert", "🟠", "🟡")) AS "",
  file.link AS "Report",
  domain AS "Domain",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signals AS "Signals"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE signal_status = "critical" OR signal_status = "alert"
SORT choice(signal_status = "critical", 0, 1) ASC, date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 20
```

---

## Signal History

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  choice(severity = "critical", "🔴",
    choice(severity = "alert", "🟠",
      choice(severity = "watch", "🟡", "⚪"))) AS "",
  file.link AS "Signal",
  signal_id AS "ID",
  source AS "Source",
  date AS "Date"
FROM "06_Signals"
SORT date DESC
LIMIT 15
```

---

## Research Feed

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  domain AS "Domain",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signals AS "Signals"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE contains(data_type, "opportunity") OR contains(tags, "opportunity-viewpoints") OR contains(data_type, "agent-analysis") OR contains(tags, "agent-analysis")
SORT date_pulled DESC, file.mtime DESC
LIMIT 15
```

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The reference canvas uses **no edges at all** — the visual hierarchy comes entirely from the colored text label nodes acting as section headers, with 900×700 file tiles spaced at 960px intervals beneath each one. This is simpler and more maintainable than a hub-and-spoke graph: you scroll vertically through sections rather than panning around a radial layout. The `color` property on text nodes maps to Obsidian's 6 built-in palette colors (1=red, 3=yellow, 4=green, 5=cyan), giving each domain a visual band.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Canvas rebuilt to match the reference style:

- **Sitdeck live embed** at top (same position as reference)
- **7 colored section bands** scrolling vertically — Entry & Orchestration (red), Orchestrator Output (red), Market Intelligence (green), Sectors & Private Markets (green), Intelligence & Research (cyan), OSINT & Media (cyan), Real World Data (yellow)
- **900×700 tiles** at 960px grid intervals, no edges
- Orchestrator README tile is red-highlighted as the hub node

rawInput.code:
import os
import re
from pathlib import Path
from collections import defaultdict

base = Path("/c/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data")

# Parse frontmatter from markdown files
def parse_frontmatter(content):
    match = re.match(r'^---\n(.*?)\n---', content, re.DOTALL)
    if not match:
        return {}
    fm_text = match.group(1)
    fm = {}
    for line in fm_text.split('\n'):
        if ':' in line:
            key, val = line.split(':', 1)
            fm[key.strip()] = val.strip().strip('"\'')
    return fm

# 1. Analyze 05_Data_Pulls for signal_status distribution
data_pulls_root = base / "05_Data_Pulls"
signal_status_counts = defaultdict(int)
pulls_by_domain = defaultdict(int)
pull_dates = defaultdict(list)
pull_frontmatter = {}

for domain_dir in data_pulls_root.iterdir():
    if domain_dir.is_dir():
        domain = domain_dir.name
        pulls_by_domain[domain] = 0
        for pull_file in domain_dir.glob("*.md"):
            content = pull_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore')
            fm = parse_frontmatter(content)
            pulls_by_domain[domain] += 1
            
            status = fm.get('signal_status', 'MISSING')
            signal_status_counts[status] += 1
            
            date_pulled = fm.get('date_pulled', 'unknown')
            pull_dates[status].append(date_pulled)
            pull_frontmatter[pull_file.name] = fm

# 2. Analyze 06_Signals for frontmatter consistency
signals_root = base / "06_Signals"
signal_ids = defaultdict(int)
signal_sources = defaultdict(int)
signal_severities = defaultdict(int)
signal_frontmatter = {}

for signal_file in signals_root.glob("*.md"):
    content = signal_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore')
    fm = parse_frontmatter(content)
    signal_frontmatter[signal_file.name] = fm
    
    sid = fm.get('signal_id', 'MISSING')
    signal_ids[sid] += 1
    
    source = fm.get('source', 'MISSING')
    signal_sources[source] += 1
    
    severity = fm.get('severity', 'MISSING')
    signal_severities[severity] += 1

# 3. Check for duplicates in 05_Data_Pulls (same date + domain + source)
duplicate_candidates = defaultdict(list)
for filename, fm in pull_frontmatter.items():
    key = (fm.get('date_pulled', '?'), fm.get('domain', '?'), fm.get('source', '?'))
    duplicate_candidates[key].append(filename)

dups = {k: v for k, v in duplicate_candidates.items() if len(v) > 1}

print("="*60)
print("SIGNAL_STATUS DISTRIBUTION (05_Data_Pulls)")
print("="*60)
for status, count in sorted(signal_status_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"  {status}: {count} files")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("FILES PER DOMAIN (05_Data_Pulls)")
print("="*60)
for domain, count in sorted(pulls_by_domain.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"  {domain}: {count} files")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("SIGNAL SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION (06_Signals)")
print("="*60)
for severity, count in sorted(signal_severities.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"  {severity}: {count} signals")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("SIGNAL SOURCE DISTRIBUTION (06_Signals)")
print("="*60)
for source, count in sorted(signal_sources.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:10]:
    print(f"  {source}: {count} signals")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("DUPLICATE/NEAR-DUPLICATE DETECTION (05_Data_Pulls)")
print("="*60)
if dups:
    for (date, domain, source), filenames in sorted(dups.items())[:10]:
        print(f"  Date={date}, Domain={domain}, Source={source}:")
        for fn in filenames:
            print(f"    - {fn}")
else:
    print("  No exact duplicates found (same date + domain + source)")

print("\n" + "="*60)
print("TOTAL COUNTS")
print("="*60)
print(f"  06_Signals files: {len(signal_frontmatter)}")
print(f"  05_Data_Pulls files: {sum(pulls_by_domain.values())}")
print(f"  Number of data domains: {len(pulls_by_domain)}")

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me synthesize this into a clean roadmap plan. Key findings:

**Pipeline Health:**
- 53 pullers, 3,242 pull files across 13 domains
- Frontmatter consistency: very good (>99% have signal_status)
- 42 thesis files properly structured
- 43 signals in 06_Signals/ — 1.3% pull-to-signal conversion

**Key Noise Problems:**
1. **Market domain dominance**: 2,419 files (74% of all pulls) — mostly daily FMP technicals tagged "clear". 

This is the biggest noise source flooding the system with routine data. Beyond that, signals never decay in weight over time, so a month-old alert carries the same importance as today's. Twelve OSINT pullers dump raw JSON without any signal scoring logic, and the company-risk-scan tool similarly lacks scoring entirely. The 1.3% conversion rate suggests either signals are being under-generated or pulls are over-generated. There's also no mechanism to amplify signals when multiple domains converge on the same theme, and agent weights are hard-coded rather than configurable.

Sports data sits isolated from equity and macro analysis, the news API routes to the wrong folder structure, and there's no lifecycle tracking for signals from initial alert through confirmation to resolution. The synthesis layer has bigger problems: no deduplication across agents, no staleness decay, no confidence compounding when multiple signals align, and no feedback mechanism where conviction re-scores the underlying agents.

I'm structuring a four-phase roadmap starting with quick wins: archiving old market technicals, adding signal scoring to those twelve OSINT pullers and the company-risk-scan tool, fixing the news routing, and implementing dashboard filters to reduce noise.

Phase 2 focuses on signal quality by externalizing agent weights to config, implementing staleness decay so older signals downgrade automatically, tracking signal lifecycle states, and promoting convergence when three or more domains align on the same thesis. Phase 3 builds the cross-domain synthesis layer with deduplication, thesis convergence multipliers, disclosure-reality integration, and a conviction delta score that measures how conviction changes over time.

Phase 4 handles operational cleanup: fixing the fred-macro group naming issue, wiring up the conviction delta scan, automating signal resolution workflows, adding retention policies to archive old clear-status pulls, and feeding sports edge scores into macro synthesis context. Now I'm structuring this as a strategic roadmap that shows both the problems and the phased solutions, with clear file dependencies for each phase.
Now I have a full picture. Let me write the roadmap plan.

content.newText:
# Plan: Data Pipeline Cleanup & Signal/Noise Improvement Roadmap

## Context

The vault has 53 pullers generating 3,242 pull files across 13 domains, feeding 43 distilled signals in `06_Signals/` and 42 thesis tracking files in `10_Theses/`. The pipeline is structurally healthy — frontmatter consistency is >99%, signal flow is bidirectional, and the Dataview dashboards work. However, three systemic problems degrade the signal-to-noise ratio and synthesis quality:

1. **Volume noise**: The Market domain alone holds 2,419 files (74% of all pulls) — mostly daily FMP technicals tagged `clear`. These flood dashboards and make true alerts harder to spot.
2. **Static signals**: No staleness decay. A 30-day-old `alert` renders identically to a fresh one. Signals have no lifecycle (new → confirmed → resolved/expired).
3. **Fragmented synthesis**: When Housing + Macro + Fundamentals all alert on the same thesis, there is no convergence multiplier. Cross-domain confirmation goes unscored. Agent weights and scoring thresholds are hard-coded.

---

## Phase 1 — Reduce Noise (Quick Wins, 1–2 days)

### 1A. Market Domain Retention Policy

**Problem:** 2,419 daily FMP technical files. Most `clear`-status files >30 days are pure noise.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/fmp.mjs`:**
- Add a `--prune` flag: archives (or deletes) `clear`-status pull notes older than 30 days from `05_Data_Pulls/Market/`
- Keep all `watch/alert/critical` notes regardless of age
- Implement in `lib/history.mjs` as `pruneStale(folder, olderThanDays, keepStatuses)`

**Command:** `node run.mjs system prune --domain Market --older-than 30 --keep watch,alert,critical`

### 1B. Add `signal_status` to 12 Unscored Pullers

**Problem:** 10 OSINT tools + `company-risk-scan.mjs` + `snscrape.mjs` write no `signal_status` field, breaking schema validation and dashboard filters.

**Fix — each file:**
- OSINT domain pullers: default `signal_status: 'clear'`; elevate to `watch` if result count > 0 (any finding), `alert` if credential breach found (`osint-leaker.mjs` already has this)
- `company-risk-scan.mjs`: map litigation count to severity (0 = clear, 1-2 = watch, 3+ = alert, active court judgment = critical)
- `snscrape.mjs`: add `signal_status` based on result volume threshold

**Files to edit:**
```
scripts/pullers/osint-amass.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-columbus.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-harvester.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-merklemap.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-octosuite.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-osmsearch.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-recon.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-spiderfoot.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-umbra.mjs
scripts/pullers/snscrape.mjs
scripts/pullers/company-risk-scan.mjs
```

### 1C. Fix `newsapi.mjs` Routing

**Problem:** `newsapi.mjs` routes output to domain folders (`05_Data_Pulls/Housing/`, `05_Data_Pulls/Government/`, etc.) based on topic keywords, not to `05_Data_Pulls/News/`. The News Agent Pulls view is empty.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/newsapi.mjs`:**
- Change output path to always write to `05_Data_Pulls/News/`
- Add `domain_hint` frontmatter field to preserve topic routing metadata
- News Agent `Pulls.md` query then works as designed

### 1D. Dashboard Freshness Filters

**Problem:** Dataview queries in dashboards don't filter by age, so stale alerts persist visually.

**Fix — `00_Dashboard/Main Dashboard.md`, `16_Agents/Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md`:**
- Priority Queue: add `AND date_pulled >= date(today) - dur(7 days)` to Active Attention views
- Signal History: add `date >= date(today) - dur(30 days)` filter to `06_Signals` query
- Mark stale entries with `(stale)` label using a computed Dataview column

### 1E. Run Vault Validator & Fix Schema Gaps

**Command:** `node run.mjs system validate`

Audit output for missing fields. Expected gaps: OSINT notes without `signal_status`, `newsapi` notes in wrong folders. Fix after 1B and 1C are deployed.

---

## Phase 2 — Signal Lifecycle (3–5 days)

### 2A. Add Signal State Tracking

**Problem:** Signals have no lifecycle. An alert that was resolved months ago still shows as `alert`.

**New frontmatter fields** (add to `lib/schema.mjs` `REQUIRED_PULL_FIELDS`):
```yaml
signal_state: new | confirmed | resolved | expired   # default: new
confirmed_date: <YYYY-MM-DD>                          # set when 2nd domain confirms
resolved_date: <YYYY-MM-DD>                           # set on resolution
resolution_note: <string>                             # why resolved
```

**New command — `scripts/pullers/signal-review.mjs`:**
- Reads `06_Signals/` and `05_Data_Pulls/` notes older than N days
- Presents unresolved alerts for manual review with `--interactive` flag
- Bulk-expires signals with `signal_state: new` older than 30 days (configurable)

**Dashboard update:** Filter all Orchestrator and Agent views to `WHERE signal_state != "resolved" AND signal_state != "expired"` or add a resolved/expired archive section.

### 2B. Staleness Decay in Dataview

**Problem:** Dashboards show all pull dates equally regardless of freshness.

**Fix — Dataview computed columns:**
- Add `daysOld` computed column: `date(today) - date_pulled`
- Color-code: >7 days = stale marker `⚠️`, >30 days = expired marker `🔴`
- Applies to: `Signal Board.md`, `Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md`, `Main Dashboard.md`

### 2C. Cross-Domain Convergence Tagging

**Problem:** When 3+ domains fire on the same thesis/ticker simultaneously, no explicit "convergence" event is created.

**New command — `scripts/pullers/convergence-scan.mjs`:**
- Reads last N days of pull notes with `signal_status: alert | critical`
- Groups by `related_theses` or ticker
- When ≥3 distinct domains flag the same thesis → writes a `convergence` signal note to `06_Signals/` with severity `critical`
- Tags: `["convergence", "cross-domain", <thesis-name>]`
- Integrates into the daily post-pull sequence in Orchestrator README

---

## Phase 3 — Synthesis Quality (1 week)

### 3A. Externalize Agent Weights & Scoring Thresholds

**Problem:** Agent weights (`Risk: 1.4, Price: 1.2`) and verdict thresholds (`> 0.18 = BULLISH`) are hard-coded in `scripts/agents/marketmind/scoring.mjs`.

**Fix:**
- Create `scripts/config/scoring-weights.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "agentWeights": { "risk": 1.4, "price": 1.2, "macro": 1.1, "fundamentals": 1.1 },
    "verdictThresholds": { "bullish": 0.18, "bearish": -0.18 },
    "downsideBoost": 1.25,
    "stalenessDecayDays": 7
  }
  ```
- `scoring.mjs` imports from config; no logic changes
- Enables per-thesis weight tuning without code edits

### 3B. Integrate Disclosure-Reality into agent-analyst

**Problem:** `disclosure-reality.mjs` generates `signal_status` but its output is never read by `agent-analyst.mjs`, leaving insider trading / material filing signals out of the synthesis verdict.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/agent-analyst.mjs`:**
- Add 8th agent: `disclosureAgent` — reads latest `disclosure-reality` pull note for the ticker from `05_Data_Pulls/Fundamentals/`
- Weight: `0.9` (informational, not predictive)
- Extract `signal_status` and `signals[]` fields → map to `{signal: 1|-1|0, confidence: ...}`

### 3C. Cross-Ticker Signal Deduplication

**Problem:** Same thesis/ticker can appear in Market Agent, Fundamentals Agent, and Sectors Agent results simultaneously with no merging.

**Fix — `scripts/lib/opportunity-viewpoints.mjs` → extend `dedupeViewpoints()`:**
- Already dedupes by ticker within a single run
- Extend to merge across domains: if same ticker has alerts from ≥2 domains, produce a merged note with `domain: cross-domain` and escalated severity
- This is already partially built (`whyUnique` field tracks domain convergence) — just needs the escalation step

### 3D. Conviction Delta Automation

**Problem:** `scan conviction-delta` exists as a command but runs manually. Conviction changes that should trigger position reviews aren't automatically surfaced.

**Fix — integrate into monthly Orchestrator cadence:**
- Add `node run.mjs scan conviction-delta` to the monthly `month-end-archive.mjs` sequence
- When delta exceeds ±2 conviction levels, auto-write a signal note to `06_Signals/` with severity `alert`
- Link to affected thesis note via `source_pull` frontmatter

---

## Phase 4 — Operational Hardening (Ongoing)

### 4A. Fix `fred-macro` Group Name Error

**Problem:** `node run.mjs pull fred-macro` fails — "Unknown FRED group: macro". Valid groups are: `housing, labor, inflation, rates, credit, liquidity`.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/fred.mjs`:**
- Add `macro` as an alias that runs all 6 groups sequentially
- OR update Orchestrator README to use explicit group names

### 4B. Auto-Archive Old Clear-Status Pulls

**Problem:** `clear`-status pulls accumulate without bound (Market domain: 2,419 files).

**Fix — `scripts/system/prune.mjs` (new):**
- `node run.mjs system prune --older-than 30 --status clear` — moves to `05_Data_Pulls/_archive/YYYY-MM/`
- Scheduled: add to `month-end-archive.mjs` sequence
- Excludes: any note referenced by a `06_Signals/` file via `source_pull`

### 4C. Sports Edge → Macro Context Bridge

**Problem:** Sports model calibration edge scores are isolated and never feed into the broader synthesis.

**Fix — `scripts/lib/macro-context.mjs`:**
- Add `getSportsEdgeSummary()` — reads latest sports-calibration pull, extracts overall model edge %
- Feed into `agent-analyst.mjs` as metadata (not a directional signal, just context: "Sports model at X% edge, baseline confidence Y")

### 4D. OSINT Signal Aggregator

**Problem:** 10 OSINT pullers fire individually per domain/target. No aggregate "OSINT threat level" for a given company or thesis.

**New command — `scripts/pullers/osint-aggregate.mjs`:**
- Groups OSINT pull notes by target/ticker within 7-day window
- Computes aggregate severity: any critical finding → critical, multiple watches → alert
- Writes a consolidated `osint-aggregate-<ticker>-<date>.md` to `05_Data_Pulls/osint/`
- OSINT Agent `Signals.md` queries this aggregated file instead of individual scan outputs

---

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] `node run.mjs system validate` — zero schema errors after Phase 1
- [ ] `node run.mjs system prune --dry-run` — shows correct files selected for archive
- [ ] Priority Queue in Main Dashboard — shows ≤20 items, all ≤7 days old
- [ ] Signal Board — all entries have `signal_state` field after Phase 2A
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull convergence-scan` — produces note in `06_Signals/` when ≥3 domains confirm
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull agent-analyst --thesis "Defense AI Autonomous Warfare"` — verdict now includes disclosureAgent
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull fred-macro` — succeeds (no group error)
- [ ] News Agent Pulls.md — shows results after newsapi routing fix

---

## Critical Files

| File | Phase | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `scripts/pullers/fmp.mjs` | 1A | Add `--prune` flag |
| `scripts/system/prune.mjs` | 1A, 4B | CREATE — retention policy runner |
| `scripts/pullers/osint-*.mjs` (10 files) | 1B | Add `signal_status` logic |
| `scripts/pullers/company-risk-scan.mjs` | 1B | Add severity scoring |
| `scripts/pullers/newsapi.mjs` | 1C | Fix output path to `05_Data_Pulls/News/` |
| `00_Dashboard/Main Dashboard.md` | 1D | Add freshness filters |
| `16_Agents/Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md` | 1D | Add freshness filters |
| `scripts/lib/schema.mjs` | 2A | Add `signal_state`, `confirmed_date`, `resolved_date` fields |
| `scripts/pullers/signal-review.mjs` | 2A | CREATE — signal lifecycle manager |
| `scripts/pullers/convergence-scan.mjs` | 2C | CREATE — cross-domain convergence detector |
| `scripts/config/scoring-weights.json` | 3A | CREATE — externalized scoring config |
| `scripts/agents/marketmind/scoring.mjs` | 3A | Import from config |
| `scripts/pullers/agent-analyst.mjs` | 3B | Add disclosureAgent (8th agent) |
| `scripts/lib/opportunity-viewpoints.mjs` | 3C | Extend dedupeViewpoints() with escalation |
| `scripts/pullers/fred.mjs` | 4A | Add `macro` alias for all groups |
| `scripts/lib/macro-context.mjs` | 4C | Add getSportsEdgeSummary() |
| `scripts/pullers/osint-aggregate.mjs` | 4D | CREATE — OSINT threat aggregator |

## Recommended Execution Order

1. Phase 1 (noise reduction) — highest ROI, improves dashboards immediately
2. Phase 2A + 2B (signal lifecycle + staleness) — prevents stale alerts accumulating
3. Phase 3A (externalize weights) — low risk, enables Phase 3B/3C tuning
4. Phase 4A (fred-macro fix) — quick, unblocks full-pull reliability
5. Phase 2C + 3B + 3C (convergence + synthesis integration) — highest complexity, do last

```
scripts/pullers/osint-amass.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-columbus.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-harvester.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-merklemap.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-octosuite.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-osmsearch.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-recon.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-spiderfoot.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-umbra.mjs
scripts/pullers/snscrape.mjs
scripts/pullers/company-risk-scan.mjs
```

### 1C. Fix `newsapi.mjs` Routing

**Problem:** `newsapi.mjs` routes output to domain folders (`05_Data_Pulls/Housing/`, `05_Data_Pulls/Government/`, etc.) based on topic keywords, not to `05_Data_Pulls/News/`. The News Agent Pulls view is empty.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/newsapi.mjs`:**
- Change output path to always write to `05_Data_Pulls/News/`
- Add `domain_hint` frontmatter field to preserve topic routing metadata
- News Agent `Pulls.md` query then works as designed

### 1D. Dashboard Freshness Filters

**Problem:** Dataview queries in dashboards don't filter by age, so stale alerts persist visually.

**Fix — `00_Dashboard/Main Dashboard.md`, `16_Agents/Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md`:**
- Priority Queue: add `AND date_pulled >= date(today) - dur(7 days)` to Active Attention views
- Signal History: add `date >= date(today) - dur(30 days)` filter to `06_Signals` query
- Mark stale entries with `(stale)` label using a computed Dataview column

### 1E. Run Vault Validator & Fix Schema Gaps

**Command:** `node run.mjs system validate`

Audit output for missing fields. Expected gaps: OSINT notes without `signal_status`, `newsapi` notes in wrong folders. Fix after 1B and 1C are deployed.

---

## Phase 2 — Signal Lifecycle (3–5 days)

### 2A. Add Signal State Tracking

**Problem:** Signals have no lifecycle. An alert that was resolved months ago still shows as `alert`.

**New frontmatter fields** (add to `lib/schema.mjs` `REQUIRED_PULL_FIELDS`):
```yaml
signal_state: new | confirmed | resolved | expired   # default: new
confirmed_date: <YYYY-MM-DD>                          # set when 2nd domain confirms
resolved_date: <YYYY-MM-DD>                           # set on resolution
resolution_note: <string>                             # why resolved
```

**New command — `scripts/pullers/signal-review.mjs`:**
- Reads `06_Signals/` and `05_Data_Pulls/` notes older than N days
- Presents unresolved alerts for manual review with `--interactive` flag
- Bulk-expires signals with `signal_state: new` older than 30 days (configurable)

**Dashboard update:** Filter all Orchestrator and Agent views to `WHERE signal_state != "resolved" AND signal_state != "expired"` or add a resolved/expired archive section.

### 2B. Staleness Decay in Dataview

**Problem:** Dashboards show all pull dates equally regardless of freshness.

**Fix — Dataview computed columns:**
- Add `daysOld` computed column: `date(today) - date_pulled`
- Color-code: >7 days = stale marker `⚠️`, >30 days = expired marker `🔴`
- Applies to: `Signal Board.md`, `Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md`, `Main Dashboard.md`

### 2C. Cross-Domain Convergence Tagging

**Problem:** When 3+ domains fire on the same thesis/ticker simultaneously, no explicit "convergence" event is created.

**New command — `scripts/pullers/convergence-scan.mjs`:**
- Reads last N days of pull notes with `signal_status: alert | critical`
- Groups by `related_theses` or ticker
- When ≥3 distinct domains flag the same thesis → writes a `convergence` signal note to `06_Signals/` with severity `critical`
- Tags: `["convergence", "cross-domain", <thesis-name>]`
- Integrates into the daily post-pull sequence in Orchestrator README

---

## Phase 3 — Synthesis Quality (1 week)

### 3A. Externalize Agent Weights & Scoring Thresholds

**Problem:** Agent weights (`Risk: 1.4, Price: 1.2`) and verdict thresholds (`> 0.18 = BULLISH`) are hard-coded in `scripts/agents/marketmind/scoring.mjs`.

**Fix:**
- Create `scripts/config/scoring-weights.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "agentWeights": { "risk": 1.4, "price": 1.2, "macro": 1.1, "fundamentals": 1.1 },
    "verdictThresholds": { "bullish": 0.18, "bearish": -0.18 },
    "downsideBoost": 1.25,
    "stalenessDecayDays": 7
  }
  ```
- `scoring.mjs` imports from config; no logic changes
- Enables per-thesis weight tuning without code edits

### 3B. Integrate Disclosure-Reality into agent-analyst

**Problem:** `disclosure-reality.mjs` generates `signal_status` but its output is never read by `agent-analyst.mjs`, leaving insider trading / material filing signals out of the synthesis verdict.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/agent-analyst.mjs`:**
- Add 8th agent: `disclosureAgent` — reads latest `disclosure-reality` pull note for the ticker from `05_Data_Pulls/Fundamentals/`
- Weight: `0.9` (informational, not predictive)
- Extract `signal_status` and `signals[]` fields → map to `{signal: 1|-1|0, confidence: ...}`

### 3C. Cross-Ticker Signal Deduplication

**Problem:** Same thesis/ticker can appear in Market Agent, Fundamentals Agent, and Sectors Agent results simultaneously with no merging.

**Fix — `scripts/lib/opportunity-viewpoints.mjs` → extend `dedupeViewpoints()`:**
- Already dedupes by ticker within a single run
- Extend to merge across domains: if same ticker has alerts from ≥2 domains, produce a merged note with `domain: cross-domain` and escalated severity
- This is already partially built (`whyUnique` field tracks domain convergence) — just needs the escalation step

### 3D. Conviction Delta Automation

**Problem:** `scan conviction-delta` exists as a command but runs manually. Conviction changes that should trigger position reviews aren't automatically surfaced.

**Fix — integrate into monthly Orchestrator cadence:**
- Add `node run.mjs scan conviction-delta` to the monthly `month-end-archive.mjs` sequence
- When delta exceeds ±2 conviction levels, auto-write a signal note to `06_Signals/` with severity `alert`
- Link to affected thesis note via `source_pull` frontmatter

---

## Phase 4 — Operational Hardening (Ongoing)

### 4A. Fix `fred-macro` Group Name Error

**Problem:** `node run.mjs pull fred-macro` fails — "Unknown FRED group: macro". Valid groups are: `housing, labor, inflation, rates, credit, liquidity`.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/fred.mjs`:**
- Add `macro` as an alias that runs all 6 groups sequentially
- OR update Orchestrator README to use explicit group names

### 4B. Auto-Archive Old Clear-Status Pulls

**Problem:** `clear`-status pulls accumulate without bound (Market domain: 2,419 files).

**Fix — `scripts/system/prune.mjs` (new):**
- `node run.mjs system prune --older-than 30 --status clear` — moves to `05_Data_Pulls/_archive/YYYY-MM/`
- Scheduled: add to `month-end-archive.mjs` sequence
- Excludes: any note referenced by a `06_Signals/` file via `source_pull`

### 4C. Sports Edge → Macro Context Bridge

**Problem:** Sports model calibration edge scores are isolated and never feed into the broader synthesis.

**Fix — `scripts/lib/macro-context.mjs`:**
- Add `getSportsEdgeSummary()` — reads latest sports-calibration pull, extracts overall model edge %
- Feed into `agent-analyst.mjs` as metadata (not a directional signal, just context: "Sports model at X% edge, baseline confidence Y")

### 4D. OSINT Signal Aggregator

**Problem:** 10 OSINT pullers fire individually per domain/target. No aggregate "OSINT threat level" for a given company or thesis.

**New command — `scripts/pullers/osint-aggregate.mjs`:**
- Groups OSINT pull notes by target/ticker within 7-day window
- Computes aggregate severity: any critical finding → critical, multiple watches → alert
- Writes a consolidated `osint-aggregate-<ticker>-<date>.md` to `05_Data_Pulls/osint/`
- OSINT Agent `Signals.md` queries this aggregated file instead of individual scan outputs

---

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] `node run.mjs system validate` — zero schema errors after Phase 1
- [ ] `node run.mjs system prune --dry-run` — shows correct files selected for archive
- [ ] Priority Queue in Main Dashboard — shows ≤20 items, all ≤7 days old
- [ ] Signal Board — all entries have `signal_state` field after Phase 2A
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull convergence-scan` — produces note in `06_Signals/` when ≥3 domains confirm
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull agent-analyst --thesis "Defense AI Autonomous Warfare"` — verdict now includes disclosureAgent
- [ ] `node run.mjs pull fred-macro` — succeeds (no group error)
- [ ] News Agent Pulls.md — shows results after newsapi routing fix

---

## Critical Files

| File | Phase | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `scripts/pullers/fmp.mjs` | 1A | Add `--prune` flag |
| `scripts/system/prune.mjs` | 1A, 4B | CREATE — retention policy runner |
| `scripts/pullers/osint-*.mjs` (10 files) | 1B | Add `signal_status` logic |
| `scripts/pullers/company-risk-scan.mjs` | 1B | Add severity scoring |
| `scripts/pullers/newsapi.mjs` | 1C | Fix output path to `05_Data_Pulls/News/` |
| `00_Dashboard/Main Dashboard.md` | 1D | Add freshness filters |
| `16_Agents/Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md` | 1D | Add freshness filters |
| `scripts/lib/schema.mjs` | 2A | Add `signal_state`, `confirmed_date`, `resolved_date` fields |
| `scripts/pullers/signal-review.mjs` | 2A | CREATE — signal lifecycle manager |
| `scripts/pullers/convergence-scan.mjs` | 2C | CREATE — cross-domain convergence detector |
| `scripts/config/scoring-weights.json` | 3A | CREATE — externalized scoring config |
| `scripts/agents/marketmind/scoring.mjs` | 3A | Import from config |
| `scripts/pullers/agent-analyst.mjs` | 3B | Add disclosureAgent (8th agent) |
| `scripts/lib/opportunity-viewpoints.mjs` | 3C | Extend dedupeViewpoints() with escalation |
| `scripts/pullers/fred.mjs` | 4A | Add `macro` alias for all groups |
| `scripts/lib/macro-context.mjs` | 4C | Add getSportsEdgeSummary() |
| `scripts/pullers/osint-aggregate.mjs` | 4D | CREATE — OSINT threat aggregator |

## Recommended Execution Order

1. Phase 1 (noise reduction) — highest ROI, improves dashboards immediately
2. Phase 2A + 2B (signal lifecycle + staleness) — prevents stale alerts accumulating
3. Phase 3A (externalize weights) — low risk, enables Phase 3B/3C tuning
4. Phase 4A (fred-macro fix) — quick, unblocks full-pull reliability
5. Phase 2C + 3B + 3C (convergence + synthesis integration) — highest complexity, do last

# Plan: Data Pipeline Cleanup & Signal/Noise Improvement Roadmap

## Context

The vault has 53 pullers generating 3,242 pull files across 13 domains, feeding 43 distilled signals in `06_Signals/` and 42 thesis tracking files in `10_Theses/`. The pipeline is structurally healthy — frontmatter consistency is >99%, signal flow is bidirectional, and the Dataview dashboards work. However, three systemic problems degrade the signal-to-noise ratio and synthesis quality:

1. **Volume noise**: The Market domain alone holds 2,419 files (74% of all pulls) — mostly daily FMP technicals tagged `clear`. These flood dashboards and make true alerts harder to spot.
2. **Static signals**: No staleness decay. A 30-day-old `alert` renders identically to a fresh one. Signals have no lifecycle (new → confirmed → resolved/expired).
3. **Fragmented synthesis**: When Housing + Macro + Fundamentals all alert on the same thesis, there is no convergence multiplier. Cross-domain confirmation goes unscored. Agent weights and scoring thresholds are hard-coded.

---

## Phase 1 — Reduce Noise (Quick Wins, 1–2 days)

### 1A. Market Domain Retention Policy

**Problem:** 2,419 daily FMP technical files. Most `clear`-status files >30 days are pure noise.

**Fix — `scripts/pullers/fmp.mjs`:**
- Add a `--prune` flag: archives (or deletes) `clear`-status pull notes older than 30 days from `05_Data_Pulls/Market/`
- Keep all `watch/alert/critical` notes regardless of age
- Implement in `lib/history.mjs` as `pruneStale(folder, olderThanDays, keepStatuses)`

**Command:** `node run.mjs system prune --domain Market --older-than 30 --keep watch,alert,critical`

### 1B. Add `signal_status` to 12 Unscored Pullers

**Problem:** 10 OSINT tools + `company-risk-scan.mjs` + `snscrape.mjs` write no `signal_status` field, breaking schema validation and dashboard filters.

**Fix — each file:**
- OSINT domain pullers: default `signal_status: 'clear'`; elevate to `watch` if result count > 0 (any finding), `alert` if credential breach found (`osint-leaker.mjs` already has this)
- `company-risk-scan.mjs`: map litigation count to severity (0 = clear, 1-2 = watch, 3+ = alert, active court judgment = critical)
- `snscrape.mjs`: add `signal_status` based on result volume threshold

**Files to edit:**
```
scripts/pullers/osint-amass.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-columbus.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-harvester.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-merklemap.mjs

scripts/pullers/osint-osmsearch.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-recon.mjs
scripts/pullers/osint-spiderfoot.mjs

scripts/pullers/snscrape.mjs
scripts/pullers/company-risk-scan.mjs
```

- Add `domain_hint` frontmatter field to preserve topic routing metadata
- News Agent `Pulls.md` query then works as designed

content.newText:
/**
 * osint-notes.mjs — Shared utility for OSINT pullers
 *
 * Writes a companion Obsidian markdown note alongside each OSINT JSON output.
 * Required for Dataview queries to surface OSINT results in dashboards.
 *
 * Signal logic:
 *   alertCount > 0  → 'alert'   (breach, dark-web, high-severity finds)
 *   resultCount > 0 → 'watch'   (any findings present)
 *   else            → 'clear'
 */

import { writeFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs';
import { dirname, basename } from 'path';

/**
 * Write a companion .md note for an OSINT scan result.
 *
 * @param {object} opts
 * @param {string} opts.tool         - Tool name (e.g. 'amass', 'spiderfoot')
 * @param {string} opts.target       - Scanned target (domain, ticker, lat/lon)
 * @param {string} opts.targetType   - 'domain' | 'ticker' | 'coordinates' | 'user'
 * @param {number} opts.resultCount  - Total findings count
 * @param {number} opts.alertCount   - High-severity finding count (0 if not applicable)
 * @param {string} opts.outputFile   - Path to the JSON file (sibling .md will be written)
 * @param {string} opts.today        - ISO date string YYYY-MM-DD
 * @param {string[]} opts.tags       - Additional tags (e.g. ['dns', 'subdomain'])
 * @param {string}  [opts.summary]   - Optional one-line summary for the note body
 */
export function writeOsintNote({ tool, target, targetType = 'domain', resultCount, alertCount = 0, outputFile, today, tags = [], summary }) {
  const signalStatus = alertCount > 0 ? 'alert' : resultCount > 0 ? 'watch' : 'clear';
  const signals = signalStatus !== 'clear'
    ? [{ id: `OSINT_${tool.toUpperCase().replace(/[^A-Z0-9]/g, '_')}`, severity: signalStatus, value: resultCount, message: `${resultCount} finding(s) for ${target}${alertCount > 0 ? ` — ${alertCount} high-severity` : ''}` }]
    : [];

  const mdPath = outputFile.replace(/\.json$/, '.md');
  const jsonRef = basename(outputFile);

  const allTags = ['osint', tool.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '-'), targetType, signalStatus, ...tags].filter(Boolean);

  const frontmatter = [
    '---',
    `title: "OSINT ${tool}: ${target}"`,
    `source: osint-${tool}`,
    `date_pulled: "${today}"`,
    `domain: osint`,
    `data_type: osint_scan`,
    `frequency: on-demand`,
    `target: "${target}"`,
    `target_type: ${targetType}`,
    `tool: ${tool}`,
    `result_count: ${resultCount}`,
    `alert_count: ${alertCount}`,
    `signal_status: ${signalStatus}`,
    `signals:`,
    ...signals.map(s => `  - id: ${s.id}\n    severity: ${s.severity}\n    value: ${s.value}\n    message: "${s.message}"`),
    ...(signals.length === 0 ? ['  []'] : []),
    `tags: [${allTags.map(t => `"${t}"`).join(', ')}]`,
    `json_ref: "${jsonRef}"`,
    '---',
  ].join('\n');

  const body = [
    `# OSINT: ${tool} — ${target}`,
    '',
    summary ?? `Passive ${tool} scan of \`${target}\`. ${resultCount} finding(s)${alertCount > 0 ? `, ${alertCount} high-severity` : ''}.`,
    '',
    `| Field | Value |`,
    `|---|---|`,
    `| Tool | ${tool} |`,
    `| Target | ${target} |`,
    `| Scan date | ${today} |`,
    `| Results | ${resultCount} |`,
    ...(alertCount > 0 ? [`| High-severity | ${alertCount} |`] : []),
    `| JSON output | \`${jsonRef}\` |`,
  ].join('\n');

  const dir = dirname(mdPath);
  if (!existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
  writeFileSync(mdPath, `${frontmatter}\n\n${body}\n`, 'utf8');
}

**1D: Dashboard Freshness Filters**

For `Orchestrator Agent/Pulls.md`:
- Priority Queue: add `AND date_pulled >= date(today) - dur(7 days)` to Active Attention views


- Add a computed staleness marker

For `Main Dashboard.md`:

- Signal History: add `date >= date(today) - dur(30 days)` filter

Looking at the Main Dashboard Priority Queue section, I need to add the 7-day filter to the existing query. But I'm realizing that filtering out older items might hide still-active alerts, so I should add a staleness indicator instead of just filtering them out completely.

The plan actually calls for two separate approaches: add a `daysOld` computed column with visual markers (`⚠️` for >7 days, `🔴` for >30 days) across all views, and then for the Active Attention views specifically, filter to only recent items. For the Priority Queue, I'll show everything but add the staleness column and sort by criticality first, then by freshness. I need to reconsider the approach here. Rather than applying a hard cutoff to the Priority Queue, I should add a staleness indicator column that flags items older than 7 days while keeping them visible. For the Watch Queue, I'll apply a stricter 14-day filter since those are lower priority. The Signal History gets a 30-day cutoff. I'm realizing the "Active Attention" language in the original plan probably refers to specific labeled views, not the entire Priority Queue itself. Now I'll implement these changes across both the Orchestrator Pulls and Main Dashboard files.

Adding a staleness indicator to the Priority Queue...

I need to reconsider how Dataview handles date comparisons. The syntax for checking if a date is older than a threshold uses subtraction with durations, so I should compare `date_pulled` against `date(today) - dur("7 days")` to flag items that are a week or older, and similarly for the 30-day threshold to mark them as stale. I'm working through the date comparison syntax for Dataview—subtracting dates gives me a duration, so I can check if something's older than 30 or 7 days to assign status indicators. Now I'm looking at the existing Main Dashboard query to see how it structures the signal status display and what data sources it's pulling from.

I need to enhance the Priority Queue table by adding a staleness indicator and sorting fresh critical items to the top, then filter the Signal History to only show recent entries from the last 30 days. Let me apply these changes now.

I have both files. Now adding the freshness filters to `Orchestrator Pulls.md` and `Main Dashboard.md` simultaneously.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Dataview supports duration math natively: `date(today) - dur("7 days")` yields a comparable date, and `(date(today) - date_pulled)` yields a duration that can be compared against `dur("7 days")`. The staleness column uses nested `choice()` so it shows nothing for fresh items — zero visual noise when signals are current.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

content.text:
All phases complete. Here's what was delivered:

**Phase 2C — `scripts/pullers/convergence-scan.mjs`**
- Scans last N days (default 7) of `alert`/`critical` pull notes across all domains
- Groups by `thesis` and `ticker` independently
- When ≥3 distinct domains flag the same key → writes a `06_Signals/` note with `severity: critical`, `signal_state: new`, and full domain attribution
- Run: `node run.mjs pull convergence-scan --dry-run` or `--days 14 --min-domains 2`

**Phase 3A — `scripts/config/scoring-weights.json` + `scoring.mjs` update**
- All magic numbers extracted: agent weights, downside boost, verdict thresholds (`0.18`), confidence formula multipliers, status classification thresholds, bearish cluster config
- `scoring.mjs` now does `createRequire` to load the JSON and references `CONFIG.*` throughout — zero behavior change, fully tunable without touching code

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Using `createRequire` in an ES module (`import.meta.url`) is the idiomatic way to load JSON in Node — it avoids the `assert { type: 'json' }` import assertion which has experimental status and differs between Node versions. The alternative (`readFileSync` + `JSON.parse`) also works but `createRequire` gives you the same caching behavior as normal `require`.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The full roadmap from the plan is now implemented. Want me to `/schedule` a weekly agent to run `node run.mjs pull convergence-scan` and post results to a signal note automatically?

The remote routine should only run the 4 steps that work purely on committed vault data. Let me update it:

You are running the daily post-pull synthesis sequence for the My_Data investment research vault.

The vault has been cloned from https://github.com/geraldfillman/reimagined-octo-adventure. All commands run from the `scripts/` subdirectory of the repo root.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT run `agent-analyst` — it requires API keys (FMP, FRED) that are not available in this environment. The agent-analyst step runs locally after data pulls.

## Step 1 — Install dependencies if needed

```bash
cd scripts
if [ ! -d node_modules ]; then npm install --silent; fi
```

## Step 2 — Run the synthesis sequence (no API keys required)

Run each command in order from the `scripts/` directory. If one fails, log the error and continue.

```bash
node run.mjs pull signal-review
node run.mjs pull convergence-scan --days 14 --min-domains 2
node run.mjs system prune --all-domains --older-than 30
node run.mjs pull opportunity-viewpoints --window 14
```

## Step 3 — Commit and push results

From the repo root, commit any new or modified files and push to origin:

```bash
git config user.email "daily-post-pull@vault"
git config user.name "Daily Post-Pull Agent"
git add 05_Data_Pulls/ 06_Signals/ 12_Knowledge_Bases/
git diff --staged --quiet || git commit -m "chore: daily post-pull synthesis $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
git push origin main
```

## Success criteria

- All 4 commands ran without fatal errors
- New convergence signals committed to 06_Signals/ if any were found
- Opportunity viewpoints updated in 05_Data_Pulls/Theses/
- Stale clear-status notes moved to 05_Data_Pulls/_archive/
- Changes pushed to origin
